reviews.review_description: I loved it I WANT MORE

I like Jellyfish Bloom and the point-cloud world in Parallel Worlds. When playing these two experiences, I set the room lighting to the faintest level that still lets hand tracking work, so that the objects glow more vividly in the dark. Additionally, in the point-cloud world, when I switch my real-life LED camping lantern to its red flashing mode, the scene transforms into a fantastical landscape that feels like being inside a cave. 

In Parallel Worlds, since the hand guidance animation appears shortly after moving between worlds, I open a small portal in the corner of my room, which lets me stay longer in the environment I prefer. (It would be even better if I could turn off the guide animation.) 

There are very few MR apps that use spatial data in such a creative way, and I hope more people will take an interest in this kind of experience in the future.
Takes advantage of scene meshing and passthrough for a compelling experience (not a game!) All it needs is dynamic occlusion to be one of the best MR apps .
This app swings open the door re the potential of MR for immersive storytelling. Three compelling experiences that were, indeed, wondrous. Hope to see more experiences added to the mix!

reviews.review_title: Good Start! Hoping For More Variety
reviews.review_description:
This was a cool experience, even though there are only three activities to take part in right now. I’m happy to support early access titles like this one that are upfront about what you’re getting. I do hope they continue updating it soon though because there is so much possibility in apps like this that immerse you in short awe inspiring experiences. It sort of reminds me of the app Liminal. 

With some additional UI additions to make it easier to navigate, and adding more diverse types of experiences this could be a winner.
Could benefit from a menu to choose experiences. Would have loved if you could actually open a portal in the parallel universes one
